zh.Philleigh, Cornwall, England: 英國英格蘭可瓦爾費爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John Le Petit於1185年出生在英國英格蘭可瓦爾費爾時，他的父親Sir John Petit of Ardevora25歲，母親Muriel Whalesborough16歲。他於1225年與Alice Seneschall在英國英格蘭可瓦爾費爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1255年6月10日在英國英格蘭可瓦爾享年70歲。

zh-hans.Philleigh, Cornwall, England: 英国英格兰可瓦爾費爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John Le Petit于1185年出生在英国英格兰可瓦爾費爾时，他的父亲Sir John Petit of Ardevora25岁，母亲Muriel Whalesborough16岁。他于1225年与Alice Seneschall在英国英格兰可瓦爾費爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1255年6月10日在英国英格兰可瓦爾享年70岁。

nameMeanings.name: Le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Petit
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Alain, Michel, Normand, Mireille, Philippe, Pierre, Achille, Armand, Jean Claude, Jean Jacques, Ketly, Adrien.

French, Walloon, Catalan, English, Jewish (from southern France), West Indian (mainly Haiti), and Mauritian: from (Old) French, Catalan, and Middle English petit ‘small’, hence a nickname for a small man (or an ironic nickname for a big man), or a distinguishing name for the younger of two bearers of the same personal name. Compare Little , Petee , and Petite .

History: Charles Petit from Anneville-Ambourville in Seine-Maritime, France, married Jeanne Rossignol (Martin and Renée Desjardins) in Quebec City, QC, in 1670. Nicolas Petit dit Lapré/Laprée from Le Gué-d'Alleré in Charente-Maritime, France, married Marie Pouponnel in Trois-Rivières, QC, in 1656. Pierre Petit dit Gobin from Lyon in Rhône married Marguerite Véron in Trois-Rivières, QC, in 1692. Pierre Petit dit Saint-Pierre from Évreux in Eure, France, married Judith Miville in Quebec City, QC, in 1758; see also St. Pierre . — This surname is listed in the register of Huguenot ancestors recognized by the Huguenot Society of America and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of South Carolina.

variants.name: Pattee
meanings: Altered form of French (Nord) Pety: variant of Petit . This surname was brought to North America from England.

variants.name: Petion
meanings: French and Haitian (Pétion): diminutive of Petit . This surname was brought to the US from Haiti, where it is by far most common.

variants.name: Petite
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Celestine, Jacques.
French: from the feminine form of   Petit   and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

variants.name: Petitt
meanings: English: variant of   Petit  .

variants.name: Pettett
meanings: English: variant of   Petit  .

variants.name: Little
meanings: English: nickname for a small man (if not ironic), or distinguishing epithet for the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, from Middle English littel, Old English lȳtel (see Light 3).
Irish: translation into English of Gaelic Ó Beagáin ‘descendant of Beagán’ (see Began ).
Americanized form (translation into English) of any of various European surnames meaning ‘little’, e.g. French Petit , Polish Mały (see Maly 1).

variants.name: Petee
meanings: Americanized form of French   Petit  .

variants.name: Pettie
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Petit  .

variants.name: Pettit
meanings: English (mainly southeastern): variant of   Petit  .

variants.name: Petito
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Carmine, Pasquale, Antonio, Rocco, Alfonzo, Alphonso, Antimo, Emilio, Giovanni, Giuseppe, Orazio, Salvatore, Silvio, Tullio, Virginio.
Italian: nickname for a small man (or an ironic nickname for a tall one), from dialect petito ‘small’ or from the personal name Petito; compare French Petit .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John Le Petit was born in 1185, in Philleigh, Cornwall, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir John Petit of Ardevora, was 25 and his mother, Muriel Whalesborough, was 16. He married Alice Seneschall in 1225, in Philleigh, Cornwall, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died on 10 June 1255, in Cornwall, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 70.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John Le Petit est né en 1185, à Philleigh, Cornouailles, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir John Petit of Ardevora, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Muriel Whalesborough, avait 16 ans. Il a épousé Alice Seneschall en 1225, à Philleigh, Cornouailles, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé le 10 juin 1255, à Cornouailles, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 70 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John Le Petit 1185 in Philleigh, Cornwall, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir John Petit of Ardevora 25 und seine Mutter Muriel Whalesborough 16. Er hat 1225 in Philleigh, Cornwall, England, United Kingdom Alice Seneschall geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb am 10. Juni 1255 im Alter von 70 Jahren in Cornwall, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John Le Petit è nato il nel 1185 nel Philleigh, Cornwall, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Sir John Petit of Ardevora, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Muriel Whalesborough, aveva 16 anni. Lui sposato Alice Seneschall nel 1225, nel Philleigh, Cornwall, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì il 10 giugno 1255, nel Cornwall, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 70 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John Le Petitが1185年にイギリスイギリスコルンワルフィレイグで生まれた時，父Sir John Petit of Ardevoraは25歳，母Muriel Whalesboroughは16歳であった。1225年にイギリスイギリスコルンワルフィレイグでAlice Seneschallと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1255年6月10日にイギリスイギリスコルンワルで70歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John Le Petit(이)가 1185년 영국 잉글랜드 코른월 필리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John Petit of Ardevora(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Muriel Whalesborough(은)는 16세였다. 그는 1225년 영국 잉글랜드 코른월 필리에서 Alice Seneschall(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1255년 6월 10일 영국 잉글랜드 코른월에서 70세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ele casou-se com Alice Seneschall em 1225, em Philleigh, Cornwall, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John Le Petit родился в 1185 г. в местности филлиг, корнвалл, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir John Petit of Ardevora было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Muriel Whalesborough, 16. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice Seneschall в 1225 г. в местности филлиг, корнвалл, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер 10 июня 1255 г. в местности корнвалл, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 70 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir John Le Petit nació en 1185, en Philleigh, Cornwall, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir John Petit of Ardevora, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Muriel Whalesborough, tenía 16 años.
